div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land clerk and lay greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God the donation which the grandfather and father of Earl Patrick as well as Earl Patrick made to them at Hartside and at Spott by the aforesaid correct bounds of the lands between the aforesaid monks and Stenton which they had in the time of the grandfather and father of the aforesaid Earl Patrick and which are named in the confirmation of the aforementioned Earl Patrick, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, and in all other things justly pertaining to the aforesaid lands; and in addition with common pasture of the moor for three flocks of rams, so that each flock should contain five hundred rams, with easements of the same moor; in addition I grant and by this my charter make firm to the aforementioned monks the five acres of land which the aforementioned Earl Patrick gave to them as an enlargement on the south side of 'Mosiburnerig' that they enclose them by ditch and bank to make a sheepfold for the aforementioned three flocks of rams; and the five acres of land may be used just as it seems best to them, yet so that they should not till them; and in addition whatever right of common the aforementioned Earl Patrick, or his heirs or his vassals, used to have or could have in the meadowland which the aforementioned monks hold of the knights of Alan fitz Walter in the feu of Innerwick, below Brunrig, which the aforementioned Earl Patrick granted to the aforementioned monks and made firm by his charter; to be held in free and pure and everlasting alms as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforementioned Earl Patrick bears witness; saving my service. As witnesses: Hugh my Chancellor; William de Morville, Constable; Philip de Valognes, Chamberlain; William Comyn; John of Hastings; Richard Marshall; Robert de Bourneville; David de la Haye; Robert de Morville. At Berwick on the sixteenth day of April.
